extractions.problem: Predicting metabolic states from gene expression data is limited; existing methods do not utilize metabolic network models to directly predict changes in flux capacity.
extractions.method: E-Flux extends Flux Balance Analysis by modeling maximum flux constraints as a function of measured gene expression, applied to mycolic acid biosynthesis and genome-scale M. tuberculosis models.
extractions.finding: E-Flux correctly predicts 7 of 8 known fatty acid inhibitors and their specificity for fatty acid biosynthesis, and identifies additional potential modulators of mycolic acid production.
extractions.dataset: M. tuberculosis, Boshoff TB expression compendium (400+ microarrays, 75 drugs/conditions)
extractions.metric: Prediction accuracy of known inhibitor identification, specificity of inhibition prediction
extractions.result: 7/8 known fatty acid inhibitors correctly predicted (87.5%)

extractions.problem: Whether physiologically relevant caffeine concentrations affect intervertebral disc cell viability and extracellular matrix composition, contributing to IVD degeneration.
extractions.method: Whole organ culture model with 190 IVD explants from rat tails in injured/uninjured groups, treated with three caffeine concentrations (5, 10, 15 mg/L) in rotating wall vessel bioreactor for 21 days.
extractions.finding: Caffeine causes significant dose-dependent loss of IVD cell viability and ECM composition, effects magnified and expedited in injured discs compared to controls.
extractions.dataset: 190 IVD whole organ explants from 16 skeletally mature Sprague-Dawley rats
extractions.metric: Viable cell density (VCD), glycosaminoglycan concentration, PG-to-HP ratio, p-value
extractions.baseline: Day 0 control IVDs (no caffeine, no injury, n=22)
extractions.result: Low vs high caffeine VCD P=.037 (day 7), P≤.004 (day 21)

extractions.problem: Determining expression patterns of low-abundance transcripts in planarians remains challenging with established WISH and FISH protocols.
extractions.method: Formamide bleaching step, optimized blocking with horse serum, copper sulfate autofluorescence quenching, iterative tyramide signal amplification, and heat-induced antigen retrieval for regenerating tissues.
extractions.finding: Formamide bleaching dramatically enhances signal intensity and sensitivity for WISH and FISH; combined modifications enable detection of previously undetectable low-abundance transcripts in planarians.
extractions.dataset: Schmidtea mediterranea (planarian), CIW4 asexual clonal line
extractions.metric: Signal intensity, signal-to-noise ratio, development time
extractions.baseline: Established planarian WISH protocol with overnight methanol/peroxide bleach
extractions.result: Dramatically reduced development time, improved signal-to-noise ratio for all probes tested

extractions.problem: Non-invasive monitoring of T-cell location and function during PDAC tumor growth and checkpoint blockade treatment is needed to predict in vivo response.
extractions.method: TbiLuc dual-color bioluminescence reporter mouse with AkaLumine substrate for red-shifted T-cell activation imaging, applied to hot/cold KPC PDAC clones with anti-PD-1/anti-CTLA-4 treatment.
extractions.finding: TbiLuc mice distinguish T-cell infiltration between immunologically hot and cold tumors; checkpoint blockade increases bioluminescence in tumor-draining lymph nodes at day 7 post-treatment.
extractions.dataset: TbiLuc mice with murine KPC PDAC clones (2838c3 'hot', 6694c2 'cold')
extractions.metric: Bioluminescence signal intensity (photons), fold induction of active vs naive T-cells
extractions.result: AkaLumine 20-fold induction in active T-cells vs 5-fold for CycLuc1

extractions.problem: How do whole cell vs LPS components of Porphyromonas gingivalis differentially modulate TLR2 and TLR4 innate inflammatory responses in periodontal pathogenesis?
extractions.method: 28 Wistar rats randomized into 7 groups receiving intrasulcular injections of P. gingivalis LPS (3 doses) or whole cell (3 doses) or placebo; TLR2/TLR4 measured by ELISA at days 0, 4, and 11.
extractions.finding: Whole cell P. gingivalis exposure increases TLR2 but decreases TLR4, while LPS exposure increases TLR4 but decreases TLR2, indicating different molecular mechanisms of pathogen recognition.
extractions.dataset: 28 male Wistar rats (8-10 weeks, 120-150g), 7 treatment groups
extractions.metric: TLR2/TLR4 serum levels (ELISA), ANOVA, paired t-test, Shapiro-Wilk normality
extractions.baseline: Placebo intrasulcular group (Group A) and day 0 pre-injection baseline

extractions.problem: Synthetic biology circuits are typically transferred to E. coli which may be suboptimal; identifying properties of alternative chassis organisms is needed for better circuit function.
extractions.method: Review of alternative microbial hosts analyzing desirable chassis properties: lifestyle, cell wall robustness, regulatory networks, whole-cell models, and tuneable metabolic fluxes.
extractions.finding: Pseudomonas putida is highlighted as a prominent alternative chassis due to metabolic diversity, robustness, and ease of manipulation; a modular chassis designed from first principles is envisioned.
extractions.baseline: E. coli as default synthetic biology chassis

extractions.problem: Existing deep learning models for object recognition are not directly applicable to microscopy images with populations of cells; whole-image classification and segmentation without single-cell annotations is needed.
extractions.method: CNN combined with multiple instance learning using a novel Noisy-AND pooling function, trained end-to-end on whole microscopy images with only image-level labels.
extractions.finding: Convolutional MIL models outperform previous methods on both mammalian breast cancer and yeast protein localization datasets without requiring any segmentation or single-cell labeling steps.
extractions.dataset: BBBC021v1 breast cancer screen (MCF-7, 12 MOA classes), yeast GFP localization (4144 strains, 17 classes), cluttered MNIST
extractions.baseline: Ensemble of 60 binary SVMs (yeast), factor analysis (breast cancer), CNN on cropped cells with bag-level labels
extractions.result: Outperforms all benchmarks on both datasets without pre/post-processing

extractions.problem: The relationship between HPV integration into host genomes and the resulting genomic structural variation in human cancers is poorly understood at high resolution.
extractions.method: Whole-genome sequencing (2×100bp paired-end, Illumina HiSeq 2000) of 10 cancer cell lines and 2 primary tumors, with breakpoint mapping and structural variation analysis at single-nucleotide resolution.
extractions.finding: HPV integrants flank and bridge extensive host genomic amplifications, deletions, inversions, and translocations; a 'looping' model explains HPV-mediated concatemer formation and focal genomic instability distinct from chromothripsis.
extractions.dataset: 10 cancer cell lines (2 cervical, 8 HNSCC) + 2 HPV+ primary HNSCC tumors
extractions.metric: Insertional breakpoint counts, viral copy number, CNV detection, PCR/Sanger verification rate
extractions.baseline: Previous SNP array-based studies of HPV integration sites
extractions.result: 111 unique insertional breakpoints in 9 HPV-positive cases; viral copy number 0-830 per cell

extractions.problem: Whether grid cell firing fields are generated by theta amplitude modulations (oscillatory interference models) or slow membrane potential ramps (attractor network models) is unresolved.
extractions.method: In vivo whole-cell patch-clamp recordings from MEC grid cells in head-fixed mice running on virtual reality linear tracks, with biocytin fills for morphological identification.
extractions.finding: Grid cells show large, reproducible membrane potential ramps tightly correlated with firing fields; theta oscillations influence spike timing but their amplitude modulations do not determine field locations, supporting attractor network models.
extractions.dataset: MEC grid cells in C57BL/6J mice, virtual 1D linear tracks
extractions.metric: Membrane potential depolarization amplitude, spatial information, correlation with firing rate, reliability
extractions.baseline: Oscillatory interference model predictions (theta amplitude modulation)
extractions.result: Ramps are larger, more reliable, and more informative about position than theta modulations

extractions.problem: Can a functional in-silico digital twin of cancer cell electrophysiology predict effects of ion channel modulation on cell cycle progression and serve as a tool for drug development?
extractions.method: Non-phenomenological digital twin of A549 lung adenocarcinoma cell electrophysiology, simulating membrane potential oscillations and effects of targeted ion channel activation/inactivation on cell cycle phases.
extractions.finding: The A549 digital twin predicts that TRPC6 blockade causes strong hyperpolarization suppressing S-G2/M transition, enabling possible cell cycle arrest; all simulated hypotheses were verified by experimental studies.
extractions.dataset: A549 human lung adenocarcinoma cell line
extractions.metric: Membrane potential (mV), cell cycle phase transition prediction
extractions.baseline: Experimental in-vivo and in-vitro verification studies
extractions.result: All simulated research hypotheses verified by experimental studies
